message:
perf(tools): word-boundary action keywords, BTreeMap reservation index, dedup thread IDs, canonicalize-once attachments

llm.rs: contains_action_keyword now enforces word-boundary matching so
substrings like "unblocked", "Next.js", or "actionable" no longer
false-positive as action items.  The new contains_any_action_keyword
helper is reused by search.rs to replace its inline keyword scan.

reservation_index.rs: Replace HashMap<prefix, Vec> exact-path storage
with BTreeMap<normalized_path, Vec> so conflict detection for exact
paths uses O(log N) lookups, ancestor walks via slash-index iteration,
and descendant scans via tight BTreeMap range queries instead of
scanning every reservation sharing the same first segment.  Glob
reservations remain HashMap-bucketed by first literal segment.  New
tests cover ancestor-conflicts-descendant and vice versa.

search.rs: parse_thread_ids now deduplicates thread IDs while
preserving insertion order via HashSet, preventing redundant SQL
queries.  summarize_messages delegates keyword detection to
llm::contains_action_keyword / contains_any_action_keyword instead of
inlining its own byte-window scan.

messaging.rs: validate_message_size_limits and
process_message_attachments now canonicalize the attachment base
directory once and pass it to
resolve_attachment_source_path_from_canonical_base, avoiding repeated
canonicalize() syscalls per attachment.  Gracefully skips attachment
size checks when the base directory does not exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
